zh.lifeSummary: 當Henry Franks於1763年6月29日出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加马里兰弗雷德里克弗雷德里克時，他的父親Michael Franks Jr.18歲，母親Elizabeth Livengood20歲。他於1786年4月與Christina Maurer Mason在美國賓夕法尼亞州法也特結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和8個女兒的父母。他在美國賓夕法尼亞州法也特赫爾曼 堂史普住了大約10年且於1801年住在美國俄亥俄州可倫比納亞。他於1781年登記服兵役。他去世於1836年5月5日在美國俄亥俄州韋恩其瓦 堂史普享年72歲，埋葬在美國俄亥俄州韋恩多勒斯托斯特努特 希爾 其特利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Henry Franks于1763年6月29日出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加马里兰弗雷德里克弗雷德里克时，他的父亲Michael Franks Jr.18岁，母亲Elizabeth Livengood20岁。他于1786年4月与Christina Maurer Mason在美国宾夕法尼亚州法也特结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和8个女儿的父母。他在美国宾夕法尼亚州法也特赫爾曼 堂史普住了大约10年且于1801年住在美国俄亥俄州可倫比納亞。他于1781年登记服兵役。他去世于1836年5月5日在美国俄亥俄州韋恩其瓦 堂史普享年72岁，埋葬在美国俄亥俄州韋恩多勒斯托斯特努特 希爾 其特利。

nameMeanings.name: Franks
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, German, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): patronymic from   Frank  .
English: perhaps sometimes a variant of Frankish, an ethnic name from Middle English frankys ‘French, Frenchman’ (Old English frencisc), for someone from France.

variants.name: Frank
meanings: German, Dutch, Scandinavian, Slovenian, Croatian, Czech, Slovak, Polish, Hungarian, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): ethnic or habitational name for someone from Franconia (German Franken), a region of southwestern Germany so called from its early settlement by the Franks, an ancient Germanic people who inhabited the lands around the River Rhine in Roman times. In the 6th–9th centuries, under leaders such as Clovis I (c. 466–511) and Charlemagne (742–814), the Franks established a substantial empire in western Europe, from which the country of France takes its name.

English (of Norman origin), Dutch, and German: from the personal name Frank (Norman French Franco, ancient Germanic Franko), in origin an ethnic name for a Frank, or from German Franke ‘Frank(ish), Franconian’ (compare 1 above). This also came to be used as an adjective meaning ‘free, open-hearted, generous’ (Middle English and Old French franc ‘free’, i.e. not a serf or slave), deriving from the fact that in Frankish Gaul only people of Frankish race enjoyed the status of fully free men. As a surname of German origin it is also found (in both possible meanings; see 1 above) in France (Alsace and Lorraine). Compare Franc and Franck .

nameMeanings.name: Henry
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: A perennially popular given name, of Continental Germanic origin, from haim ‘home’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was an Old French name, adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. It has been borne by eight kings of England. Not until the 17th century did the form Henry (as opposed to Harry ) become the standard vernacular form, mainly under the influence of the Latin form Henricus and French Henri.

historicEvents.description: Thomas Jefferson's American Declaration of Independence endorsed by Congress. Colonies declare independence.

historicEvents.title: A Free America
historicEvents.description: The Revolutionary War ended with the signing of the Treaty of Paris which gave the new nation boundries on which they could expand and trade with other countries without any problems.

historicEvents.title: Albany is Named Capital of New York
historicEvents.description: Albany became the capital of New York in 1797. Albany is the oldest continuous settlement of the original 13 colonies.

historicEvents.title: Financial Relief for Public Land
historicEvents.description: A United States law to provide financial relief for the purchasers of Public Lands. It permitted the earlier buyers, that couldn't pay completely for the land, to return the land back to the government. This granted them a credit towards the debt they had on land. Congress, also, extended credit to buyer for eight more years. Still while being in economic panic and the shortage of currency made by citizens, the government hoped that with the time extension, the economy would improve.

historicEvents.title: Slavery Becomes Illegal in New York State
historicEvents.description: During the years 1799 to 1827, New York went through a period of gradual emancipation. A Gradual Emancipation Law was passed in 1799 which freed slave children born after July 4, 1799. However, they were indentured until 25 years old for women and 28 years old for men. A law passed 1817 which freed slaves born before 1799, yet delayed their emancipation for ten years. All remaining slaves were freed in New York State on July 4, 1827.

en.lifeSummary: When Henry Franks was born on 29 June 1763, in Frederick, Frederick, Maryland, British Colonial America, his father, Michael Franks Jr., was 18 and his mother, Elizabeth Livengood, was 20. He married Christina Maurer Mason in April 1786, in Fayette, Pennsylvania, United States. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 8 daughters. He lived in German Township, Fayette, Pennsylvania, United States for about 10 years and Columbiana, Ohio, United States in 1801. He registered for military service in 1781. He died on 5 May 1836, in Chippewa Township, Wayne, Ohio, United States, at the age of 72, and was buried in Chestnut Hill Cemetery, Doylestown, Wayne, Ohio, United States.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Henry Franks nasceu em 29 de junho de 1763, em Frederick, Frederick, Maryland, British Colonial America, seu pai, Michael Franks Jr., tinha 18 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Livengood, tinha 20 anos. Ele casou-se com Christina Maurer Mason em abril de 1786, em Fayette, Pensilvânia, Estados Unidos da América. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 8 filhas. Ele viveu em German Township, Fayette, Pensilvânia, Estados Unidos da América por quase 10 anos e em Columbiana, Ohio, Estados Unidos da América em 1801. Ele alistou-se no serviço militar em 1781. Ele faleceu em 5 de maio de 1836, em Chippewa Township, Wayne, Ohio, Estados Unidos da América, com 72 anos, e foi sepultado em Chestnut Hill Cemetery, Doylestown, Wayne, Ohio, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Henry Franks родился 29 июня 1763 г. в местности фредирикк, фредирикк, Мэриленд, бритиш колоняль амирика, его отцу по имени Michael Franks Jr. было 18 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Livengood, 20. Он женился на женщине по имени Christina Maurer Mason апрель 1786 г. в местности феиетт, Пенсильвания, Соединенные штаты. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 8 дочерей. Он жил в местности джирман тоуншип, феиетт, Пенсильвания, Соединенные штаты около 10 лет и в местности колумбяна, Огайо, Соединенные штаты в 1801 году. В 1781 году он поступил на военную службу. Он умер 5 мая 1836 г. в местности чиппюа тоуншип, веин, Огайо, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 72 лет и был похоронен в местности честнут хилл кеметири, доилестоун, веин, Огайо, Соединенные штаты.
